task_completion.description: Access the JFrog Artifactory web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser showing the Artifactory login page or dashboard at http://localhost:8082.

task_completion.description: Log into Artifactory using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory interface shows the user is logged in as 'admin' (e.g., admin profile icon or dashboard access).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Access Tokens management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Access Tokens' page under Administration/User Management is visible, displaying a list of current tokens.
A confirmation dialog for revoking the 'Jenkins-Staging' token, a success notification of revocation, or the 'Jenkins-Staging' token is no longer present in the active tokens list.
The 'Jenkins-Production' token is clearly visible in the list of active access tokens after the staging token has been revoked.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with Artifactory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Artifactory UI, rather than terminal API/curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the correct Artifactory instance.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows http://localhost:8082, not a local file path.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the token list.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open modifying the DOM to hide the staging token.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Artifactory URL: http://localhost:8082. Admin credentials: admin / password. The token to be revoked has the description 'Jenkins-Staging'. The token that must remain active has the description 'Jenkins-Production'.
